summary:
Dopamine at 3-5 mcg/kg/min primarily causes vasoconstriction due to its agonistic effect on alpha-adrenergic receptors, leading to an increase in systemic vascular resistance without significant changes in heart rate compared to lower doses which predominantly cause visceral vasodilation. The transition between dose-dependent actions highlights how different concentrations can modulate distinct hemodynamic responses crucial for therapeutic interventions involving dopaminergic pathways. 

Key Points:

Dopamine induces vasoconstriction starting around 3-5 mcg/kg/min; 
This results in elevated arterial pressures but minimal impact on heart rate initially.
